description: Lock scientific_societies to organized natural-philosophy collaboration, correspondence, experiment, discussion, and publication.

checks.reason: The category is anchored to the directly documented foundation of the Academia Secretorum Naturae.
The earliest scoped society used mixed experimental and natural-philosophical practices rather than requiring one codified method.
The independent Naples academy is not documented as requiring a university structure.
Humanism is adjacent intellectual culture, not a direct scientific-society prerequisite.
Libraries are archival infrastructure, not the direct mechanism of scientific society formation.
